name: Patrick Dwyer – NewEdge Wealth
text_content:
Contact overview

Name: Patrick J. Dwyer
Role: Partner / Managing Director, NewEdge Wealth
Company: NewEdge Wealth
Primary email: pdwyer@newedgecg.com

This contact was created from a Gmail thread titled "Moj and Pat", where Patrick Finnegan introduced Moj to Patrick Dwyer (NewEdge Wealth) as a potential LP / capital partner for Kinship Ventures.

Origin of contact

Intro source: Warm introduction from Patrick Finnegan (Second Sight Ventures & Intuition Capital), who suggested that Pat would be a strong fit as an LP in Moj and Gwyneth’s fund.
Initial thread:
Patrick Finnegan emailed Moj and Pat to connect them, recommending that Pat consider becoming an LP in Kinship’s fund.
Pat replied expressing interest in connecting and asked to find time to meet.
Moj responded with a detailed overview of Kinship Ventures Fund I & II, including investment focus, track record, and fund terms, and asked Gaby to help coordinate scheduling.

Latest email context (Feb 9, 2026)

In the latest reply, Jenny from Moj Office emailed Patrick with two concrete options for a first call:
Wed, Feb 11, 2026 at 10:00am PT (1:00pm ET)
Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 10:00am PT (1:00pm ET)
The thread notes that Pat can work around Moj’s schedule and references recent market/investment content from NewEdge that Pat shared (links in his signature to their video library and 2025 views deck).
The conversation is focused on setting up an LP intro / relationship meeting, not yet on specific commitment sizing.

Use this task to track the relationship with Pat / NewEdge Wealth as a potential LP / wealth advisory partner to Kinship, including:

Status of the first Zoom/phone meeting and any follow‑ups.
Pat’s interest level, questions, and diligence asks on Fund II.
Any eventual allocation, commitment structure, or ongoing co‑investment or advisory collaboration.

Follow‑up calendar notifications show an updated invitation reflecting the confirmed time window and Zoom details.

name: Allocate – Hana Yang
text_content:
Contact overview

Primary contact: Hana Yang (CXO & Co‑Founder)
Company: Allocate
Primary email: hana@allocate.co
Context: Relationship around Allocate’s GP/LP "Beyond Summit 2026" and portfolio Insights product demo for Kinship.

Origin of contact

This contact was created from a Gmail thread titled "Re: Follow up on Demo and Beyond Summit 2026" between Hana and Moj, where Hana followed up after a prior conversation about:

Scheduling a demo of Allocate’s Insights product for Kinship’s portfolio tracking.
Inviting Moj (and potentially Trina and Gwyneth) to speak at Beyond Summit 2026, an invite‑only GP/LP event focused on the innovation economy.
Requesting data room access for Kinship so Allocate can review materials and provide feedback.

In the most recent email in the thread, Hana:

Asks whether Kinship can provide an indication by next week or early the following week about participating in the Summit content.
Confirms they aim to wrap all Summit content around that timing and would like to solidify Kinship’s role.
Reiterates that the team is aligned on moving forward with the demo and next steps around the data room.

Relationship summary

Allocate is a potential platform and ecosystem partner for Kinship (portfolio tracking, Insights tooling, and GP/LP Summit visibility).
There is ongoing back‑and‑forth with Hana on content and participation for the 2026 Summit and on giving Allocate access to Kinship’s data room.

Use this task to track ongoing coordination with Hana / Allocate, including:

Confirmation of Kinship’s participation and session concept for Beyond Summit 2026.
Status of the Insights product demo for the Kinship portfolio.
Any future capital‑raising, co‑investment, or distribution opportunities that may emerge from the relationship.

name: Ara / Mechanism of Action (MOA)
text_content:
Mechanisms-based health innovation
Early diligence required to assess scientific thesis, team strength, and defensibility.


Founder: Ara Katz
Meeting Date: Nov 29
Raise: $5M–$7.5M on a $45M pre-money valuation
Stage: Pre-launch / Pre-commercial with active B2B demand
Data Room Link: 

1. COMPANY OVERVIEW

MoA (Mechanism of Action) is a translational science and design studio turning frontier pharmaceutical R&D into clinically validated consumer and B2B products.
The platform includes:

Premium consumer brand (MoA-branded ingestibles + topicals)
B2B licensing + ingredients engine
Innovation + IP studio (novel compounds, delivery systems, patents)


Their core identity: proof-driven, IP-protected health performance innovations spanning supplements, functional foods, topicals, and delivery technologies.



2. VISION & PRINCIPLES (from the microsite)
Vision:
Engineering frontier R&D into a new canon of human performance and optimization.
Core Principles:

The body is not a market.
Breakthroughs are made in control groups, not focus groups.
Design is delivery.
Products should perform, not promise.
Aiming to reset the baseline for how humans live, age, recover, and perform.




3. TEAM + KEY CONTRIBUTORS
MoA is built by a high-caliber scientific and commercial team with 100+ years combined experience in:

pharma
biotech
clinical research
performance nutrition
scaled consumer health
Innovation + Product Team

Anthony Almada, M.Sc., FISSN — Cofounder, CIO

Pioneer of creatine in North America
90+ clinical collaborations / 60+ product launches

William Wallace, Ph.D. — Cofounder, CSO

Leading science communicator and supplement developer

Raja Dhir — Advisor, Innovation
Tye Jensen — Advisor, Innovation
Ivan Wasserman, Esq. — Advisor, Regulatory
Commercial + Brand

Ara Katz — Cofounder, Brand
Tessa Mu — Cofounder, Marketing
Will Mayer — Cofounder, Creative

Key Functions / Partners

Ingredients: Indena, CellMark, PLT
Testing: SMRTL, Alkemist Labs
Development: Mercenary Group, Prof. Zaworotko (Limerick University)
Regulatory/Quality: Tim Stewart, Ph.D.
Clinical Trials: People Science, Lindenwood University, Texas A&M
Manufacturing: NutraSelf
Tech/Diagnostics: Pheno.AI, SpotDx




4. PRODUCT + TECHNOLOGY PIPELINE
MoA is developing a mix of branded consumer products and B2B ingredients/technologies.
B2B (2025 Launches)


CKMT1™ Creatine Monohydrate (Q4 2025)

Ultra-pure, ultra-fine 400-mesh
Only creatine that can be dosed up to 20g while staying under CA Prop 65 limits
Already significant inbound interest and early purchase order demand

Pharma-grade Cocrystal Delivery Technology (Q4 2025)

In-licensed patents
Creates superior solubility and bioavailability
Positions MoA as a foundational ingredient supplier to multiple categories


Consumer (MoA Originals)


Q2 2026: High-Dose Creatine Supplement (built around CKMT1™)
Q2 2026: Omega-3 Complex (bio-native phospholipid profile; blood-brain barrier penetration via LPC-DHA)
Q3 2026: Daily Ready-To-Mix (men, women, menopausal women) using cocrystal delivery
Q1 2027: Topical Skin Treatment (cocrystal-delivered dermatological compounds)
Q1 2027: Hair & Scalp System (enzymatic pathway targeting with novel botanical extract)




5. BUSINESS MODEL
MoA operates a dual-engine model:

1. B2B Licensing & Ingredients (Early Profitability)


High-margin ingredient supply
White-label formulations
Licensing of proprietary delivery systems
Partners include major sports orgs, children’s performance brands, and formulators

2. Premium Consumer Portfolio (Brand + Recurring Revenue)


Clinical-grade ingestibles and topicals
Limited-release, scarcity-driven approach
Amazon presence early
International from day one (UAE & UK relationships activated early)

This structure provides:

early cash flow from B2B
premium margins and long-term value from DTC




6. CLINICAL STRATEGY

MoA is intentionally built with peer-reviewed potential and real-world efficacy:

Academic partnerships (Texas A&M, People Science, CAHS)
Clinical endpoints beyond typical supplement metrics
Prospective trial registration
Systemic biomarker tracking
Designed for publication, not marketing claims
This is notable because very few consumer health companies pursue actual clinical validation.



7. IP + DEFENSIBILITY
MoA’s defensibility includes:

In-licensed patents for cocrystal technology (use + composition)
Novel proprietary compounds (CKMT1™)
100× solubility and 30× bioavailability delivery systems
Continuous innovation pipeline
Strong regulatory and clinical infrastructure
This positions MoA more like a biotech platform applied to consumer health, not a traditional supplement brand.



8. FINANCIALS + PROJECTIONS


First three SKUs project 70%+ gross margins
Path to profitability within 12–24 months
Early B2B demand reduces CAC pressure
Capital primarily going to IP, platform development, clinical research, and GTM




9. CURRENT FUNDRAISE


Raising: $5M–$7.5M
Valuation: $45M pre-money
Already soft-circled: ~$6M (strategic angels + early investors)
Developed intentionally before outreach; this is the first external raise
Ara is selectively bringing in operators and strategic partners




10. WHY THIS MAY BE INTERESTING FOR KINSHIP

It blends scientific IP, clinical validation, and brand design
Strong fit with our interest in performance health and human optimization
The B2B engine aligns with our thesis about infrastructure rather than trends
Their approach to design + science aligns with your operating lens
MoA is intentionally building a home for creators, KOLs, and experts who want rigor and legitimacy




DATA ROOM

Q2 2026: Omega-3 Complex (bio-native phospholipid profile; blood-brain barrier penetration via LPC-DHA)
Q3 2026: Daily Ready-To-Mix (men, women, menopausal women) using cocrystal delivery
Q1 2027: Topical Skin Treatment (cocrystal-delivered dermatological compounds)
Q1 2027: Hair & Scalp System (enzymatic pathway targeting with novel botanical extract)




5. BUSINESS MODEL
MoA operates a dual-engine model:

1. B2B Licensing & Ingredients (Early Profitability)


High-margin ingredient supply
White-label formulations
Licensing of proprietary delivery systems
Partners include major sports orgs, children’s performance brands, and formulators

2. Premium Consumer Portfolio (Brand + Recurring Revenue)


Clinical-grade ingestibles and topicals
Limited-release, scarcity-driven approach
Amazon presence early
International from day one (UAE & UK relationships activated early)

This structure provides:

early cash flow from B2B
premium margins and long-term value from DTC




6. CLINICAL STRATEGY

MoA is intentionally built with peer-reviewed potential and real-world efficacy:

Academic partnerships (Texas A&M, People Science, CAHS)
Clinical endpoints beyond typical supplement metrics
Prospective trial registration
Systemic biomarker tracking
Designed for publication, not marketing claims
This is notable because very few consumer health companies pursue actual clinical validation.



7. IP + DEFENSIBILITY
MoA’s defensibility includes:

In-licensed patents for cocrystal technology (use + composition)
Novel proprietary compounds (CKMT1™)
100× solubility and 30× bioavailability delivery systems
Continuous innovation pipeline
Strong regulatory and clinical infrastructure
This positions MoA more like a biotech platform applied to consumer health, not a traditional supplement brand.



8. FINANCIALS + PROJECTIONS


First three SKUs project 70%+ gross margins
Path to profitability within 12–24 months
Early B2B demand reduces CAC pressure
Capital primarily going to IP, platform development, clinical research, and GTM




9. CURRENT FUNDRAISE


Raising: $5M–$7.5M
Valuation: $45M pre-money
Already soft-circled: ~$6M (strategic angels + early investors)
Developed intentionally before outreach; this is the first external raise
Ara is selectively bringing in operators and strategic partners




10. WHY THIS MAY BE INTERESTING FOR KINSHIP

It blends scientific IP, clinical validation, and brand design
Strong fit with our interest in performance health and human optimization
The B2B engine aligns with our thesis about infrastructure rather than trends
Their approach to design + science aligns with your operating lens
MoA is intentionally building a home for creators, KOLs, and experts who want rigor and legitimacy

name: Sandra Soskic - Humanoid Labs
text_content:
Humanoid Labs introduction
Humanoid Labs, created by former Apple executives and backed by investors behind Peloton, Fitbit, Madison Reed, Zoom, Substack, Alchemy, and Ethereum, is creating a new category at the intersection of self-expression, identity, and community: skinwear, a patented system fusing NFC-enabled color cosmetics, adaptive AI, and materials innovation.
We’re building the infrastructure for how people express themselves across physical and digital worlds. Our connected ecosystem removes the usual barriers of skill, confidence, and time, turning self-expression into an adaptive, data-driven experience that drives deeper engagement and diversified revenue across products and community experiences.
Now in public beta, Humanoid is showing early organic traction across sales, engagement, and retail interest. Our IP-protected hero product launches May 2026.
[read more here]


here is the link to our deck containing the additional information.

name: SLAM BioTherapeutics
text_content:
Contact Name: Rick Heinick
Email: rheinick@slambio.com
Company: SLAM Bio / SLAM BioTherapeutics, Inc.
Role/Title: CEO & Chairman; Founder & Chairman
LinkedIn/Website:   SLAM Biotherapeutics+1
Situation Summary:
Rick emailed Moj to schedule a call for next week (Monday or Tuesday) to discuss SLAM Bio’s progress and potential investment participation; he included a non-confidential deck and detailed company overview in the body of the email. Moj replied asking him to resend everything in a clean, fresh email summarizing who they are, what they’re doing, where the company is, and what they’re looking for so she can bring Gwyneth into the loop. 
Relevant Context:  (max 5 bullets)
SLAM Bio is an oncology biotech company developing a platform of first-in-class antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and bispecific antibodies targeting the SLAM (Signaling Lymphocytic Activation Molecule) family of immune receptors to treat blood cancers (AML, MDS, CTCL, FL) and other life-threatening malignancies. SLAM Biotherapeutics+2Ein News+2
The lead candidate, SLM1224 (ADC), targets SLAMF5 (CD84) and has shown strong preclinical efficacy and safety; they anticipate entering the clinic around 2026, supported by a $10M seed round and a subsequent ~$35M Series A financing for IND-enabling studies and clinical development.
Rick’s email notes SLAM Bio recently closed a $10M seed round in June and a $35M Series A in September and is in active discussions with multiple investors to finalize a lead for the Series A. 
The leadership team includes co-founder Dr. Steven Rosen (City of Hope) and collaborators at the Weizmann Institute; SLAM Bio holds an exclusive license to its therapeutic antibodies and is building a broad IP portfolio.
Moj emphasizes the need to be respectful of Rick’s time and requests a concise summary email clarifying company status and ideal asks from Kinship/Trolley to streamline bringing Gwyneth into the conversation. 
Action Needed:
Wait for Rick’s “fresh” summary email and updated materials; once received, review the deck and financing details, then decide whether to (a) schedule the proposed call next week and (b) internally assess SLAM Bio as a potential oncology/ADC investment or advisory opportunity to brief Gwyneth accordingly.

text_content:
Contact Name: Jonathan (Jon) Coe
Email: jon@awct.us
Company: Acoustic Wave Cell Therapy, Inc. (AWCT)
Role/Title: President & CEO
LinkedIn/Website:   awct.us
Situation Summary:
After briefly meeting Moj at a conference, Jon followed up via email with a deck and overview of AWCT’s acoustic pulse therapy platform for women’s pelvic health. Moj asked him to resend a “clean” email summarizing company status and goals so she can loop in the broader Kinship team; Jon replied confirming he has done this. 
Relevant Context:
AWCT is a Bay Area–based medical device company developing a non-invasive acoustic pulse therapy platform that activates tissue repair mechanisms without drugs, implants, or surgery. awct.us+1
The company’s initial focus is on women’s pelvic floor disorders—particularly stress urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse—with clinical studies showing efficacy comparable to surgery but with lower risk. awct.us+2awct.us+2
AWCT was founded to commercialize UCSF-developed technology from Dr. Tom Lue’s lab, backed by decades of NIH/DOD-supported research and a substantial patent portfolio. awct.us+1
Jon is a serial medtech entrepreneur and currently President/CEO of AWCT; he previously co-founded and led Prescient Surgical and serves as a strategic advisor to several healthtech companies. THE ORG+1
AWCT is in late clinical development and working toward commercialization and Series C financing to scale its women’s health offering. Fogarty Innovation+1
Action Needed:
Await and review Jon’s “clean” follow-up email and attached deck (with clear business details, current stage, and goals), then decide whether to (a) schedule the suggested follow-up meeting next week to dig into clinical data, regulatory path, and commercialization strategy, and (b) route the opportunity internally as a potential women’s health / pelvic floor medtech investment or advisory fit.

name: Hone Health
text_content:
Meeting Context Snapshot
Contact Name: Saad Alam
Email: salam@honehealth.com
Company: Hone / Hone Health
Role/Title: Co-Founder & CEO
LinkedIn/Website:   /   Hone Health
Situation Summary:
Saad has shared a Hone Health data room and initial materials with Moj for potential investment/advisory involvement. Moj replied with a detailed follow-up email outlining governance, cap table, economic waterfall, marketing/CAC, and founder-alignment questions; Saad plans to refine answers and update the data room over the coming days. 
Relevant Context:
Saad is the Co-Founder & CEO of Hone Health, a telehealth / longevity platform focused on hormone optimization and proactive, diagnostic-driven care. Hone Health+1
Hone recently completed a ~$33M Series A and positions itself as one of the leading telehealth clinics for hormone and longevity care. AlleyWatch+1
In the thread, Saad shares that the data room currently includes: operating model/P&L, Subscriber Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis, retention by line of business, cap table, and a “Primary Care is Dead” article framing the market shift. 
Moj’s follow-up email organizes diligence into four buckets: (1) governance/ownership/structure (GC $90M CAC facility, warrants, founder ownership & voting, cap table accuracy), (2) waterfall modeling & distributions at various exit values, (3) marketing/CAC & growth performance (channel-level CAC, TikTok + Mario Lopez partnership, creative org), and (4) broader founder perspective (protective provisions, board mechanics, founder control & succession, information rights, employee/options and advisor/celebrity equity structures). 
Saad notes the data room was assembled quickly while the company is “on a rapid sprint to scale internal infrastructure before double marketing spend in Jan,” and that he will make further edits to the materials. 
Action Needed:
Internally review the data room and Moj’s diligence question list, prioritize the highest-conviction/clarity gaps (cap stack, GC facility mechanics, warrants, economic waterfall, and CAC by channel), then coordinate a Zoom with Saad to walk through those points live and confirm whether Kinship wants to progress toward a formal advisory/investment proposal.

name: Lovable AI
text_content:
Meeting Title: Follow-up Call – Moj & Mindaugas (Lovable.dev Allocation)
Participant: Mindaugas Petrutis
Personal & Career Background
Co-Founder of Lovable.dev – platform merging product, dev, and design culture.
Founder/CEO of Coho, a private network for design leaders.
Previously led growth/community initiatives at InVision and design recruiting.
Active voice in the design/product space — frequent writer and speaker on career strategy and authenticity in creative industries.
Current Updates / Recent Moves (90 Days)
July 31: Introductory Zoom between Kinship Ventures, Mindaugas, Anton, and Lovable team. Focus: alignment + partnership potential.
Post-call: Moj expressed strong interest in a strategic investment and larger allocation for Kinship LPs.
Sept 25: Hosted Lovable x Will.i.am buildathon & mixer in LA — Moj attended in person; Gwyneth unable to attend but expressed enthusiasm.
Mindaugas has been updating Kinship team on team status, investment structure, and follow-up opportunities.
Current stage: Moj is pressing to finalize allocation, citing “several million dollars in committed interest” and requesting expedited next steps.
How to Engage Mindaugas
Lead with relationship momentum: Reference Moj’s presence at the LA mixer and shared excitement.
Be clear and decisive on allocation ask — he’ll appreciate specificity and seriousness.
Highlight Kinship’s founder-first thesis and alignment with Lovable’s community-led ethos.
Frame Kinship as more than capital: strategic amplification, cultural cachet, LP network leverage.
Keep meeting outcome-oriented: “How do we secure Kinship’s allocation and set next steps for close?”
Potential Objections / Sensitivities
Allocation pressure: other investors may be circling, so he’ll want clarity on Kinship’s speed + process.
May want to ensure Moj and Kinship bring strategic partnership beyond money.
Watch for bandwidth: Lovable team is mid-build and mid-raise; keep asks tight.
Closing & Business Development Opportunities
Finalize Kinship Allocation in Lovable.dev
Be explicit: confirm Kinship’s desired allocation size (multi-million range).
Lock timeline: align on closing mechanics + paperwork to avoid losing ground to other investors.
Strategic Partnership Beyond Capital
Offer LP amplification (Kinship LPs can be early users, evangelists, distribution nodes).
Position Moj + Gwyneth as high-visibility cultural allies who can open doors beyond capital.
Future Collaboration Hooks
Co-create moments like the Hollywood buildathon — Moj could help convene One Table or Kinship network events with Lovable as partner.
Explore product/cultural intersections (Lovable + Kinship-backed founders in design/creator economy).
✅ Meeting Goal for Moj: Cement Kinship’s allocation in Lovable.dev, position Kinship as a strategic, not just financial partner, and walk away with clear next steps to close.

Platform processes $4bn+ in gross payment volume and is one of the fastest growing payments businesses
In 2025 they acquired Oosto which extends its solutions into security and commerce beyond the vehicle
Blue chip customers include: Aeroporti Di Roma, Chevron, Morgan Stanley, Ford, Deloitte, Honeywell, Fifa World Cup, and Adani

name: Bread Beauty
text_content:
11/06 - Fathom link
Notes
Kinship Ventures <> Bread Beauty : Fearless Fund - November 05

VIEW RECORDING - 25 mins (No highlights)
Meeting Purpose
Discuss potential investment and restructuring options for Bread Beauty, a beauty brand facing financial challenges despite strong growth.
Key Takeaways
Bread Beauty is experiencing significant growth (up to $2.6M projected revenue for 2024) but facing urgent fundraising challenges
A recap (recapitalization) is being considered as the primary option, with soft landing/acquisition as a backup
Moj advised creating a detailed recap proposal with a waterfall analysis to present to current and potential investors
Emphasis on protecting founder/team wellbeing and maintaining incentives throughout the process
Topics
Company Overview and Current Situation
Bread Beauty has raised ~$7M to date, with founders retaining 40% ownership
2024 projected revenue of $2.6M, up significantly from $1.2M in 2023
Strong performance in Sephora and Ulta, with expansion plans for 2025
Facing urgent need for capital to sustain growth and reach profitability
Funding Challenges and Options
Previous fundraising attempts unsuccessful, including a lead investor pulling out
Currently exploring recapitalization (recap) as primary option
Soft landing/acquisition possibilities being explored in parallel
Urgency to make decisions "in days, not months"
Recap Strategy and Advice
Moj advised creating a detailed recap proposal including: 
Waterfall analysis of current cap table and proposed changes
Debt restructuring plans
New investment terms (e.g., $2-3M at new valuation)
Founder/executive incentives (e.g., carve-outs)
Importance of addressing all stakeholders: founders, team, consumers, and investors
Suggestion to present a clear path to profitability with the new funding
Industry Context and Founder Support
Acknowledgment of widespread challenges in the beauty/consumer goods space
Emphasis on protecting founder mental health and considering all options
Offer of additional support and connections from Moj, regardless of investment decision
Next Steps
Bread Beauty team to prepare detailed recap proposal and waterfall analysis
Send proposal to Moj for review and potential investment consideration
Explore additional investor options if needed, with Moj offering to make introductions
Continue parallel discussions on soft landing/acquisition possibilities
Prioritize timely decision-making given the urgent financial situation
Action Items
Prep & send recap proposal to Moj. Inc: waterfall, debt details, new $ amt, valuation, cap table impact, founder/exec incentives, performer w/ revenue projections, burn rate, team info. Coordinate w/ Gillis on financials. - WATCH (30 secs)

Fearless Fund
text_content:
11/16 - Fathom meeting
Notes
Fearless Fund (Recap Call) - November 15

VIEW RECORDING - 44 mins (No highlights)
Meeting Purpose
Discuss restructuring strategies for portfolio companies 13 Lune and Bread Beauty Supply facing financial difficulties.
Key Takeaways
Both 13 Lune and Bread Beauty Supply are facing cash flow issues and need immediate restructuring
Debt restructuring and cap table reorganization are critical for both companies' survival
Management team retention and incentivization is crucial for successful turnarounds
Fearless Fund is willing to support restructuring efforts with additional capital ($250k-$500k for 13 Lune)
Topics
13 Lune Situation
$24M revenue last year, but facing cash flow issues due to JCPenney relationship changes
Has $3M+ in senior secured debt that needs restructuring
Aiming to raise $3-4M in new capital
Management team (Patrick, Nyakio) feeling stressed and overwhelmed
Restructuring Strategy for 13 Lune
Convert all existing shares (preferred and common) to common stock
Raise new capital at a lower valuation ($6M pre-money suggested)
Restructure senior secured debt to be on par with other creditors
Potentially implement management carve-out or increased equity ownership
Target $3M raise with $1-1.5M already identified from insiders
Bread Beauty Supply Challenges
Lower margins due to retail-first business model
Less investor interest compared to 13 Lune
Marcy Ventures (major investor) resigned from board
Restructuring Approach for Bread
Determine minimum cash needed for 15-24 months of runway
Consider drastic valuation cut (potentially to $3-4M) to incentivize new investment
Focus on management team retention and excitement post-recap
Target beauty industry operators/founders as potential investors
Investor Communication Strategies
Create detailed cap table waterfalls to show ownership pre/post restructuring
Provide specific debt details and restructuring plans
Be transparent about company challenges and turnaround plans
Emphasize management team retention as key priority
Next Steps
Sam to send 13 Lune financial models and restructuring proposal to Moj
Fearless Fund to have follow-up call with Patrick (13 Lune) to discuss restructuring details
Request detailed cap table waterfalls and debt information from both 13 Lune and Bread
Moj to share BeautyCon restructuring documents as reference
Schedule follow-up meeting next week to review progress
Plan Q1 2024 educational session on startup restructuring best practices
Action Items
Send 13 Loon financial models to Moj - WATCH (30 secs)
Draft proposal for 13 Loon restructure - all common stock, $6M valuation, $3M raise, debt renegotiation - WATCH (30 secs)
Schedule Q1 Zoom w/ Moj re: company turnaround strategies - WATCH (30 secs)
Send BeautyCon waterfall + final memos to Sam/Jazmin privately - WATCH (30 secs)
Schedule call w/ Patrick (13 Loon) to discuss restructure proposal, get waterfall - WATCH (30 secs)
Contact Bread for waterfall, valuation, debt details - WATCH (30 secs)

Attendees:
jazmin@fearless.fund
sam@fearless.fund
ariansimone@fearless.fund

name: L-Nutra
text_content:
11/15 - Fathom meeting
Notes
L-Nutra <> Kinship Ventures - November 14

VIEW RECORDING - 13 mins (No highlights)
Meeting Purpose
Explore potential investment and strategic partnership opportunities between Kinship Ventures and L-Nutra.
Key Takeaways
L-Nutra operates two businesses: El Nutra (Prolon fast-mimicking diet) and Nutrition for Longevity (medically tailored meals)
El Nutra revenue ~$60M this year; shifting focus to B2B partnerships (employers, health providers, retail)
Nutrition for Longevity at $31.3M pre-money valuation, with $10M still open; leveraging Medicaid/Medicare reimbursements
Kinship Ventures (Moj & Gwyneth Paltrow's fund) interested; flexible investment options (cash, equity, phantom equity, warrants)
Topics
L-Nutra Business Overview
El Nutra: Prolon fast-mimicking diet + expanding product suite (protein shake, chocolate spread, bar)
Nutrition for Longevity: Medically tailored meals, founded by Walter Longo
El Nutra revenue: ~$60M this year, up 23% YoY despite GLP-1 weight loss drug competition
Shifting strategy: D2C → B2B partnerships (employers, health providers, retail)
New partnerships: Equinox, nearing agreement with Exponential Fitness
El Nutra Health Initiatives
L-health.com: Metabolic program + diabetes regression/remission program
Preserve lean muscle mass (side effect of Prolon)
Example: Imperial Hat (96 employees) - $1M/yr Ozempic costs vs. $105K for L-Nutra's program
4 clinical trials with promising results (not universally applicable)
Nutrition for Longevity Details
Founded 2019 to sell longevity diet
Expanded into Medicaid/Medicare reimbursed meals
$31.3M pre-money valuation, $10M still open
~$4,500 annual revenue per patient
0.4M people eligible for reimbursed meals in the U.S.
Investment Opportunities & Needs
El Nutra: Cash injection + retail strategy for non-Prolon products
Nutrition for Longevity: Cash + strategy for integrating longevity diet (employers/consumers)
Kinship Ventures offers flexible investment options: cash, equity, phantom equity, warrants
Personal Connections
Moj Mahdara: Long-time friend of Josh (Michael's cousin), partners with Gwyneth Paltrow in Kinship Ventures
Michael Lawsky: Joined L-Nutra in 2022 to help organize and scale
Previous Goop collaboration mentioned (details unclear, but seemingly successful)
Next Steps
Michael to send comprehensive decks on L-Nutra portfolio and businesses
Moj to review materials and consider investment/partnership opportunities
Potential exploration of marketing strategy collaboration with Josh
Further discussion on specific needs and investment structure once decks are reviewed
Action Items
Send Moj deck on growth strategy, El Nute portfolio info, updated business info for both L-Nutra & Nutrition for Longevity - WATCH (30 secs)
Review L-Nutra & Nutrition for Longevity info from Michael, consider investment/partnership options - WATCH (30 secs)

name: Ceremonia
text_content:
11/6 - Fathom link here
Notes
Moj x Babba - November 05

VIEW RECORDING - 45 mins (No highlights)
Meeting Purpose
Discuss potential recapitalization strategies for Ceremonia and next steps.
Key Takeaways
Ceremonia is considering a recapitalization due to challenging market conditions
Multiple financing scenarios and valuation options need to be modeled out
External investor involvement likely needed to execute recap successfully
Data room and financial projections should be prepared to facilitate process
Topics
Current Situation
Ceremonia doing $5M revenue this year but facing financing challenges
Babba has invested ~$500K personally via convertible note
Multiple beauty/CPG companies currently recapitalizing in tough market
Recapitalization Approach
Lawyer provided initial recap model, but needs refinement
Finance person should create 3 scenarios at different valuations (e.g. $6M, $8M, $12M)
Valuation likely shouldn't exceed $10M (2x revenue)
Could potentially recap to all-common cap table with no preferred stock
New investors will want significant ownership; existing investors may be diluted
Potential Investment
Kinship Ventures (Moj) could potentially invest $1-2M as part of recap
Other potential investors mentioned: Kirsten Green, Susan Lyon, Ariane Samoan, Founderland
Next Steps
Schedule call with Moj and Ceremonia finance person for Thursday/Friday
Finance team to create 3 recap scenarios at different valuations
Babba to send current investor deck to Moj for review
Moj to compile list of potential co-investors to approach
Prepare data room and financial materials for investor outreach
Action Items
Instruct finance person to create data room w/ 3 recap scenarios, term sheet framework, projections, burn rate, debt info - WATCH (30 secs)
Email Moj to schedule 30-min call w/ finance person for Thu. Include Lador. - WATCH (30 secs)
Send Moj link to current company resume/deck - WATCH (30 secs)
